P0.definition: Blocks a core clone workflow end-to-end (operator cannot complete critical task).
examples: Cannot add indexers due to incompatible definition contract.
Release grab fails to enqueue torrent at runtime.

P1.definition: Major operator impairment with severe operational impact but partial workaround exists.
examples: Metadata search works only with undocumented key assumptions.
Subtitle operations work for one variant path only.

P2.definition: Important parity gap; does not block core workflow but materially reduces fidelity.
examples: Missing diagnostics/remediation hints on indexer failures.
Wanted list filtering inconsistent with source clone behavior.

P3.definition: Polish, consistency, or quality debt with low immediate operator risk.
examples: Minor copy inconsistencies across UI surfaces.

classificationRules: Choose highest-severity user-impacting failure observed in runtime verification.
Downgrade only when a verified workaround preserves end-to-end operation.
Raise one level when issue affects multiple clone domains simultaneously.
Mark REGRESSION when behavior previously verified as parity-complete no longer works.
